name: Custer Federal State Bank
description_short: Community bank with locations in Kearney and Broken Bow, Nebraska, offering checking, savings, mortgages, agricultural loans, and business financing including SBA 7(a) loans.
description_long:
Custer Federal State Bank is a state-chartered community bank operating in Nebraska with branch locations in Kearney and Broken Bow. The institution emphasizes local decision-making and personalized service, positioning itself as a neighborhood financial partner with deep roots in the communities it serves. The bank was founded on the principle of understanding local needs and maintaining quick loan approval processes for its customer base.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking products. For consumers, these include checking accounts (including KASASA accounts with 2% cash back on debit purchases), savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and HSA accounts. On the lending side, Custer Federal provides home mortgages tailored to various buyer profiles, agricultural loans leveraging FSA programs, SBA 7(a) business loans for equipment purchases and construction financing, and personal loans. Digital services include mobile banking with fund transfers, mobile deposit capabilities, online banking, and online bill pay.

Custer Federal distinguishes itself through its agricultural lending focus and FSA program utilization, reflecting its rural Nebraska market. The bank holds a 5-star rating from Bauer Financial, indicating strong financial health. Customer service is emphasized as a core value, with named staff members (e.g., mortgage officer Katrina) mentioned on marketing materials. The institution combines traditional banking infrastructure—with ATMs available 24/7 and drive-up windows—with modern digital conveniences like mobile banking and mobile deposit.

As a regional community bank, Custer Federal is best suited for customers in its service areas (Kearney and Broken Bow) seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank appears to operate only two physical locations, making it inaccessible to customers outside these Nebraska communities. Customers requiring nationwide branch networks or extensive service centers should consider larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: KASASA checking accounts with 2% cash back on debit purchases
Home mortgages for purchase and refinancing
Agricultural loans with FSA program options
Mobile banking app with transfers and mobile deposit
pros: KASASA checking account offers 2% cash back on debit card purchases with no penalties
5-star Bauer Financial rating indicates strong institutional financial health
Specialized agricultural lending with FSA program utilization for farm operations
SBA 7(a) loan program for business expansion, construction, and equipment purchases
Mobile banking app with fund transfers, mobile deposit, and debit card management
Quick loan decision-making process for agricultural and business customers
cons: Limited to two physical branch locations (Kearney and Broken Bow, Nebraska only)
No evidence of extended weekend or evening hours—lobby closes at 4:00 p.m. weekdays
Website contains no pricing information, APR ranges, or fee schedules for comparison
No mention of online account opening or remote banking onboarding for non-local customers
Limited product transparency—CD rates, mortgage rates, and loan terms not displayed on website
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farm operators in central Nebraska seeking specialized lending
Small business owners in Kearney or Broken Bow needing SBA 7(a) financing
Local homebuyers seeking relationship-based mortgage underwriting in rural Nebraska
Consumers in the bank's service areas preferring community banking over national chains
diagnosis: Custer Federal State Bank is best for agricultural operators, small business owners, and homebuyers in its Kearney and Broken Bow, Nebraska service areas who value community banking relationships and local loan decision-making. The primary limitation is strict geographic restriction to two branch locations, making it inaccessible to customers outside central Nebraska and unsuitable for those seeking nationwide banking infrastructure.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank in Kearney and Broken Bow, Nebraska offering checking, savings, mortgages, and agricultural loans.
